transcript: Hey, yeah, this is Smitty Dealio.
Hey, Smitty! Are you the driver that’s going to pick that load up today?
Okay, awesome! So it’s at, uh, the freight terminal—
—and it’s gonna need to get, ah, picked up.
Whe—When are you lookin’ to do this? [exhales]
Uh, today! One second; let me get on the phone with Devin.
—Here, stay on the phone for a sec.
CBD Vendors, how can I help you?
Hey, Devin, this is, ah, Smitty, and he just called me about the pickup today. So I was just tellin’ him it’s gonna be today at the central freight terminal and to come grab this freight today.
—What, ah—Yeah, I mean, we’re all set, man! The driver was dispatched and we should be good to go!
And I think this is your driver, I think, Devin. Am I mistaken, Smitty? You’re the driver, right?
Alright, perfect. Do you have the terminal number on the BOL?
I’ll make sure—th—the dispatcher should have it. I’ll ah—
[Todd, a Good Vibe CBD employee, enters call]
—Lemme talk to the dispatcher. Let—Let’s make sure we’re on the same page here.
Okay, who am I—Which—Who am I speaking with?
With Good Vibe CBD. You called us. What do you want?
Good Vibe CBD? What are you talkin’ about? We just—
—We just got—You just got a call from—You’re the Denver, Colorado number that just called us named Smitty, right?
Um, we missed a call from this number; I called you back.
Oh my god! No, I don’t—I haven’t called anyone in Denver—
Hey, it’s Smitty! I got cut off. Sorry about that.
Oh, okay! What happened? Some guy came on the line? That was the weirdest thing ever!—
—No, my line just went dead. I wasn’t sure what even happened.
—I was like, “Hello?! Anybody there? Hello?” Okay, so I’m in the process of rounding this up—

—Smitty, are you on the line now, Smitty? Because this is Devin. I don’t know what the hell just happened on that call. That was, like—I feel like my phone is fuckin’—I don’t know if the gover—I don’t know what the fuck’s goin’s on!
Okay, I don’t know if someone’s bugging me, man. I don’t know.
Smitty, I do have a question for you; are you with, ah, Fab [?] Service, LLC?
Okay, perfect! I just wanna make sure. So, Jamie is your dispatcher, correct?
Alright, perfect! Yeah, we’re good to go. Um,I—I sent the BOL to Jamie, so if you have any questions in regards to pickup addresses or anything like that, please contact your dispatcher, okay?
Oh, so just go to them with any further inquiries, you’re saying?

Yes, sir! Your dispatcher should have all the information; we’re all good. She said that you’re about 30 minutes out and I believe I’m tracking you guys now. Uh, looks like update within Aurora, Colorado. So, we’re go—we’re all good to pickup, man. If you could get there ASAP, please do, alright?
[A female employee of Good Vibe CBD, enters call]
Jackson, it’s me again. How you doin’?
Good, bro. Is your—Smitty, are you using like a scrambler or something to call me? ‘Cause my phone is being weird—
—Like a burner service or something, yeah?
No [delay] No. Anyway, I was just gonna see if I could possibly get a little CBD, just for my own personal sesh.
Oh, is this—Are you gonna be driving the load today?
Yeah! I just mean whenever it’s convenient. Something I was interested in.
Yeah! I’m so confused right now. I don’t even know what’s going—But yeah, the—Sure, yeah! Of course! Absolutely—
—No problem! Text me an address of wherever you’d like me to send it. I can send you a little bit.
See, do you hear that person coming in right now? No?
Yo, Jackson. Ya with me? Hello?
Hey, man, your phone is just—There’s somethin’ wrong with your phone.
I don’t know what the problem is.
And I actually—This is your number? This is even better because I can text you the info for the pickup. Are you gonna go pick that up in Denver right now?

Okay, great! I’ll text you, because it seems like the longer we stay on the phone—It literally had somebody else pick up the phone and join the call. It’s ju—I don’t know what is going on. It’s the strangest thing I’ve ever had happen, so—
—No, I lost you completely. And I was like, “Hello?” So [delay]—
—Yeah, start—It’s starting again, right now. Yeah, it’s actually starting again right now. It’s starting to crackle and do the same thing—
Talk to you soon. Thank you so much!
Yeah, today’s order isn’t actually CBD, but we are a CBD company. So if you just text me an address, I’ll get that sent to you, okay?
Smitty Dealio. Do you know when I could expect payment?
Uh, did—Did you pick up the load?
The freight terminal never gave you the load though, right? Didn’t they—Didn’t the close?
No, I did what I was supposed to do up ‘ere! I never got any CBD or any payment.
You went to the freight line?
You—Didn’t you sit there for quite some time? Is that right?
And then did they end up giving it to you?
I didn’t get any money from them, no.
No, I—The freight. Did they give you the freight?
Yeah! And I dropped it off and I did everything up ‘ere! [exhales] [delay]. Look, you promised me CBD and, uh, and a payment. That’s alls I’m trying to find out about here.
Yeah! What I’m confused about—what I’m trying to find out about—is that the load wasn’t picked up. So that’s what I’m trying to do, so you can do what you’re trying to do. Does that make sense?
No, I completed everything on-time and hundred percent!
—That’s how I like to operate. I don’t like to leave loose ends hanging or any stragglers or anything like that.
Yeah, ‘cause I was told that the—they wouldn’t release it to you. The—the freight line. Does that sound familiar?
No, it doesn’t. So, you’re not gonna pay me, or what?
No, I—I think you’re confusing me with somebody, because you’re—So what’s the load? Do you have the paperwork in front of you?—
—Hey, this is Smitty Dealio! I mean, I talked to you fair ’n square!
—D’you remember talking to me about this? I went along with ya and I did step-by-step.
I talked to you and I talked to Devin, I talked to Jackson! I talked to Devin; I talked to the freight people; I talked to everybody I was supposed to! [delay]
Okay! Do you have the proof of delivery? Do you—
Can you—Can you send it over to me?
See, I didn’t get that. You keep saying that you sent it. You’re talking—
—Big man! Oh, I sent it last week! I was waiting to give ya plenty of time to prepare my payment—
—And now you’re saying, “Oh, I never got it”. I mean, what do I have to do here?
You’re talking about Devin. You keep switching the people you’re talking about. Devin. You said that—
—Oh, I’m now swithing anything, guy. I did my job!—
—You’re not talking to Devin. You’re not talking to Devin right now.
And I worked ‘round the clock, guy! I worked up ’n down; I worked ‘round the clock for you people, is what I did! [exhales] [delay]—
—I don’t own the freight—I don’t own the freight line. You have to talk to Devin for payment. I don’t know what’s going on with that. He’s the freight guy—
—Just listen: Just give me a hundred bucks, boom, we’re done! I’ll call it good, that’s it! I don’t need to bother ya any further. What do you say?
No, I don’t handle any of the payment—
—It’s not anything to do with me. So, what are you trying to do?
You were gonna give me money and CBD, is what you were gonna do. And I don’t have anything here. I was drivin’ up ’n down and gong up ’n down the stairs and everything. [exhales]
Oh, you’re talking about for the boxes? Yeah!
—Hey, you gotta relax; you need to relax. Devin is the one that pays you for the freight. I talked to you last week. I said I would send you some CBD—
—Oh, bigtime! [delay] I had to pull a pulley; I had to get onto an assembly line, just ‘cause I wanted to lend a hand!—
—‘Cause I’m professional! Y’know, I’m a professional. And when they said they need a hand in there, I lent ‘em a hand! You know, what do you want from me? [exhales] [delay]
I want you to send me the proof of delivery and send me your address; you never sent that to me.
I sent everything I was supposed to send and this guy’s havin’ me pull ropes and I had to pull a pulley, I had to get on a ladder, I had to do everything!
You need to relax! You’re not hearing what I’m sayin’.
Oh, I hear ya! I know I don’t have any money, I don’t have any CBD; I don’t have anything I’m supposed to have! [delay] [inhales & exhales]
—Look, just meet me and give me a hundred dollars: Boom! End of story, we’re done! [exhales]
You still haven’t sent me the POD. You’re talking about Devin and now you’re calling me, asking for money—
I’m not the freight company. I am not the freight company—
—I’m going to hang up the phone.
Hello? How may I help you?
Hey, hello? How may I help you?
’S, ah, Smitty Dealio. What’s happenin’ here?
Hey, what’s up, man? How are you?
I’m hangin’ in there, brother. I’m just tryin’ to collect on this monetary thing we had going here last week.

Yeah, so the thing is, you never—I was talking about a shipment in Colorado and I think you’re calling me about a shipment, like, a long time ago, maybe like two months ago and I’m not a freight company—
—so you’re confusing me and calling me, because you didn’t—
—you didn’t pick up—you didn’t pick up four pallets this last—
—Yes, I did! I don’t think you understand what’s going on here!—
—Where’s the POD? Then send me the POD—

—When I got to the freight place—Listen to me: When I got to the freight place, they had me walkin’ dogs over there, I had to help paint a wall; they were puttin’ me to work at this frickin’ place here!
Paint a wall? What are you talking about? You’re not even making sense—
—I had to climb on a ladder. They’re like, “gimme a hand here!” They were doing—
—Yeah, you’re pissing me off. You keep calling me and you’re not making sense. Send me a POD—
—PODor send me your MC number. You don’t have anything. You just keep calling me and harassing me. Quit calling me!
